title: Book-keepers, payroll managers and wages clerks
description: Examine, analyze, and interpret accounting records to prepare financial statements, give advice, or audit and evaluate statements prepared by others. Install or advise on systems of recording costs or other financial and budgetary data. Examine budget estimates for completeness, accuracy, and conformance with procedures and regulations. Analyze budgeting and accounting reports. Prepare tax returns for individuals or small businesses. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Process and issue employee paychecks and statements of earnings and deductions, including verifying attendance, hours worked, and pay adjustments, and posting information onto designated records.
Compile employee time, production, and payroll data from time sheets and other records, and distribute and collect timecards each pay period.
Review time sheets, work charts, wage computation, and other information to detect and reconcile payroll discrepancies, ensuring accurate payroll processing and record keeping.
Keep informed about changes in tax and deduction laws, provide information to employees and managers on payroll matters, and prepare and file payroll tax returns.
Keep track of leave time for employees and process paperwork for new employees, entering their information into the payroll system.
Record employee information, such as exemptions, transfers, and resignations, to maintain and update payroll records, including payroll adjustments for previous errors or retroactive increases.
Compute wages and deductions, enter data into computers, and resolve discrepancies in accounting records.
Prepare detailed reports on audit findings, including investigating financial records and transactions to ensure compliance with laws and regulations.
Examine records and analyze financial and information systems to recommend necessary controls and ensure system reliability and data integrity; confer with company officials about financial matters.
Audit payroll and personnel records for compliance with tax laws and prepare adjusting journal entries as necessary.
Analyze business operations, financial commitments, and obligations, and provide advice on budgeting and financial planning.
Prepare or assist in preparing simple to complex tax returns for individuals or small businesses, ensuring compliance with relevant tax laws.

Interview clients to obtain additional information on taxable income, deductible expenses, and allowances, and furnish taxpayers with sufficient information and advice to ensure correct tax form completion.
Review financial records and documentation of expenditures to determine forms needed to prepare tax returns, checking figures and data for accuracy.
Perform bookkeeping tasks such as posting data and checking billing data for accuracy, while ensuring all transactions are recorded following proper procedures.
Collect and analyze data to detect deficiencies, fraud, or non-compliance with laws and regulations, and report to management on financial discrepancies and audit results.
Monitor and reconcile payroll accounts, bank transactions, and ensure proper operation of equipment used in payroll processing.
Develop, implement, modify, and document recordkeeping and accounting systems, while ensuring compliance with company policies and regulations.

Organize or work with detailed office or warehouse records, using computers to enter, access, search or retrieve data. Maintain files, document transactions, and assist in preparing forms and ensuring accuracy of information.
Review insurance policies to determine coverage, investigate errors, and verify applicant information, including credit and personal references, to ensure compliance with regulations.

Process, prepare, and submit business or government forms and loan applications, ensuring accuracy and completion. Interview clients to gather necessary information and assist with claims or account openings.
Provide customer service by answering inquiries, guiding clients on claims, and referring them to appropriate personnel when needed.
Check value of collateral for loans, calculate premiums or fees, collect payments, and record transactions using computers, ensuring accurate document maintenance.
Review customer accounts for compliance with loan terms, analyze credit information, and prepare reports regarding transactions and account status, ensuring proper management of financial records.
Assist in scheduling, coordinating closings of mortgage transactions and ensuring that all necessary documents are collected and complete during this process.
Handle clerical tasks such as filing, typing, operating standard office machines, and general office maintenance as part of operational support.
Perform other administrative tasks such as maintaining records, typing correspondence, and ensuring accuracy of applications and financial documents as needed.

Enter information into databases or software programs.
Prepare documentation for contracts, transactions, or regulatory compliance.

Distribute materials to employees or customers.
Maintain current knowledge related to work activities.
Calculate costs of goods or services.
Prepare financial documents, reports, or budgets.
Discuss account status or activity with customers or patrons.
Answer telephones to direct calls or provide information.
Search files, databases or reference materials to obtain needed information.
Obtain personal or financial information about customers or applicants.
Respond to customer problems or complaints.
Interview employees, customers, or others to collect information.
Monitor equipment operation to ensure proper functioning.
Weigh parcels to determine shipping costs.
Maintain office equipment in proper operating condition.
Report maintenance or equipment problems to appropriate personnel.
Report information to managers or other personnel.
Discuss business strategies, practices, or policies with managers.
Correspond with customers to answer questions or resolve complaints.
Provide notifications to customers or patrons.
Interview clients to gather financial information.
Identify opportunities to improve operational efficiency.
Evaluate effectiveness of personnel policies or practices.
Prepare cash for deposit or disbursement.
Develop business or financial information systems.
Convert data among multiple digital or analog formats.
Testify at legal or legislative proceedings.
Advise others on human resources topics.
Advise others on business or operational matters.
Determine the value of goods or services.
Discuss goods or services information with customers or patrons.
Confer with coworkers to coordinate work activities.

emerging_tasks.task: Communicate financial reports and budgets to stakeholders.

Human Resources Administrative Assistant (HR Administrative Assistant)
